DryUI Terms

Last updated: April 11, 2026

DryUI is distributed as open source software. Unless a separate written agreement says otherwise, use of the source code and published packages is governed by the repository license.

Open source license

The DryUI repository is licensed under the terms published in the root LICENSE file.

No warranty

DryUI is provided on an “as is” basis. You are responsible for evaluating whether it is fit for your use case and for testing any generated code, plugin workflows, or MCP integrations before production use.

Third-party services

If you use DryUI alongside editors, package registries, or AI tooling platforms, your use of those services remains subject to their own terms.

Contact

Questions about these terms can be directed to rob@dryui.dev.
